description: Skip think for tasks where user tells exactly what to change
Combine verify+done in same turn — model got confused
Skip codebase_context for small repos — model needs it
Relax done gate — convergence gate is necessary
Encourage batch calls — broke prompt formatting
SWE prompt 'be FAST' — iteration reduction but quality loss (5/6→3/6)
Include FAIL_TO_PASS test names in SWE prompt — agent reads tests first
Strip Task Management, Skills, Memory sections in headless mode
Remove RPI warning injection — cost extra iterations for explicit tasks
After edit tool succeeds, tell model 'edit succeeded, call done now'
hypothesis: Many iterations are wasted on post-edit verification. If we tell the model the edit worked, it can skip re-reading.
replace: ACT — use `edit`, `write`, `bash` to make changes. When an edit succeeds, the harness confirms it. You can trust the confirmation and call `done` without re-reading.

description: Use grep to find the line, then read only that section
hypothesis: Reading whole files wastes tokens. grep→read(offset) is more efficient.
find: Use `batch` to read multiple files in one call.
replace: Use `grep` first to find the exact line, then `read` with offset/limit to see only the relevant section. This saves tokens. Use `batch` to combine multiple searches.

description: For tasks with explicit instructions, skip read and edit directly
hypothesis: When user says 'change X to Y in file Z', no need to read first.
find: Start by reading relevant files to understand the codebase, then make changes.
replace: If the task tells you exactly what to change (file, old text, new text), edit directly. Only read first when you need to discover what to change.
